title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
At a colonial-era house, a detective delivers a large envelope to Inspector Vance, whose expression turns stern upon reading the contents. Helen Bredwood interrupts, demanding an explanation regarding Stephen’s health. Vance dismisses her concerns, noting that Stephen mentioned groin pain, and Helen leaves to find Dr. Temple, who is currently sailing on a steamboat.

Vance and a detective proceed to a small cottage to interrogate Fox, the gardener and chauffeur, who is revealed to be Phil Pendleton. Vance confronts Pendleton with his criminal record, which includes theft at Vandervliet State Prison in Illinois, assault in California, and breaking and entering in Michigan. Pendleton maintains his innocence regarding the murder of Thomas Brad and expresses a desire to go straight. 

Pendleton confesses that an old prison associate tracked him down and threatened to reveal his criminal past to his employer, Mr. Brad, unless Pendleton met with a contact in New York. Pendleton claims he went to New York on Tuesday night, where a thug made him a criminal offer that he refused. While Pendleton refuses to disclose the specific details of the offer or confirm if he met Patsy Malone, Vance agrees to allow him to remain under police surveillance rather than in formal detainment, promising not to reveal his past to Mrs. Brad if he is innocent.

On Sunday morning at 9:15, while staying at Bradwood, Vance receives a confusing phone call from Stollings. After a brief, cryptic exchange with the caller, Vance hurries out of the house.
